title: verbascum graecum
intro: Verbascum graecum, also known as Greek mullein, is a biennial plant species in the figwort family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Verbascum graecum include alleviating coughs, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations, while its herbal preparations typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the leaves and flowers. In addition, the plant's roots can also be used to create a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
